FADE · Storyboard · Script × Frames

The Bookie

Flagship cut — Sal, in sequence
RUNTIME  ~60 sec
MASTER  9:16
BEATS  8

One flagship assembly, read top to bottom: the line on the right, the frame that carries it on the left. Lines are pulled from Sal's bank — trim, swap, or reorder freely. Frames are previs, matching the look, not the final footage.

A1 · WS
Static · 35mm
BEAT 01 · OPEN
The book
"I took bets for thirty years. You called me, I wrote it down. I knew everybody's record before there was a record."
Direction
Settled, feet up, faintly amused. He's not bragging — he's just telling you how it was.
A2/A3 · MCU
Static · 50mm
BEAT 02 · TURN
Now they ask me
"The kids ask me for picks now. Me. The bookie. You spend your whole life on one side of the phone — now they want you on theirs."
Direction
Dry, a little delighted by the irony. The wonder, not the grievance.
A4 · CU
Static · 85mm
BEAT 03 · THE READ
I had the records up here
"The app shows me everybody's record. I had the records up here before there was a here."
Direction
A small tap at the temple. Calm certainty — he's not impressed with himself, it's just true.
B2 · ECU
Static · macro
BEAT 04 · THE FADE
Before he's done tapping
"Tail the sharps, fade the Tonys. Took me thirty years to learn it — the app teaches it on the loading screen. I get the notification, I fade him before he's done tapping."
Direction
Cut to the hands. He taps "fade" without looking down. VO over the insert.
A2/A3 · MCU
Static · 50mm
BEAT 05 · THE APP
Four minutes
"My grandson set it up in four minutes. Took me thirty years to build what this thing does on the loading screen. I'm not bitter. I'm impressed."
Direction
Genuine wonder. This is the heart of it — he loves the new world.
B3 · ECU
Static · opt 48fps
BEAT 06 · REAL MONEY
One of those you can feel
"A number going up ain't the same as a man counting hundreds onto your kitchen table. One of those you can feel."
Direction
VO over the cash insert. The first turn toward what's lost — still warm, not bitter.
B4 · MS → A4 · CU
Static · 35mm
BEAT 07 · THE DOOR
Nobody comes to the door
"I press 'cash out' and I keep waiting for somebody to come to the door. Nobody comes to the door no more. I miss the door."
Direction
Hold on the door, then cut to his face. Wistful — and it passes. He'd never ask for your pity.
A4 · CU
Slow push · 85mm
BEAT 08 · CLOSE
I believe a phone now
"Forty years I trusted nobody. Now I believe a phone. That's the world."
Direction
A small knowing smile. Hold the beat, then cut to the FADE card.
End card
